layout_note: Single column. Sections divided by a thin double 'brick-course' rule (the structural signature - two hairlines a few px apart, like a mortar joint between brick courses, echoing the older-home brickwork Amrock works on). Boldness spent once on the typographic 'older homes brought up to standard' hero + the single fired-terracotta accent; everything else quiet. Typographic-led, not photo-led - only one usable finished photo exists, so it is featured in the body rather than the hero.

sections.copy_intent: Text wordmark 'Amrock Electrical'; nav: Work, Reviews, Areas; one button 'Get a quote'. No phone in the bar on mobile.
sections.visual_intent: Quiet solid header (no backdrop-filter so the mobile drawer/scrim are not trapped - containing-block rule), brick-course hairline underneath. Mobile: wordmark + hamburger only (no phone, <=2 links) per mobile_header check.

sections.purpose: State trade + location + the older-home angle in one line; surface the only citable proof. Typographic (no photo) - the deliberate hero decision (see design-direction.md and build-notes).
evidence_anchors: reviews[0] Molly (older property, additional sockets, light fittings, car charger)
sections.copy_intent: Headline = trade-led positioning on the older-home angle (NOT a review quote). Subhead = older-home upgrades (sockets, lighting, storage heaters, electric fires) + EV chargers, explained as you go, + 5.0/27 proof. CTAs: Get a quote (primary, #quote), Call Naz (secondary, phone).
sections.visual_intent: Typographic hero on the --ink brown-black ground with a faint terracotta-tinted brick-course texture (CSS only) and a short terracotta rule under the eyebrow. Heavy Inter Tight headline; terracotta primary button; small proof chip '5.0 on Google, 27 reviews'. Text block keeps the site container side padding on mobile (hero horizontal-inset rule); CTAs stack full-width <=640px, proof chip below both. NO stock/AI image.

sections.purpose: Surface the evidence-drawn proof points in a seamless loop directly under the hero.
evidence_anchors: reviews (older homes, explained clearly, storage heaters & electric fires)
sections.copy_intent: 4-6 factual proof points: 5.0 on Google, 27 local reviews, Cardiff CF23, Older homes brought up to standard, Explained as we go, EV chargers fitted. No exclamation marks, no salesy framing.
sections.visual_intent: --ink band; terracotta diamond separators after every item (covers the wrap seam); single full traversal in ~36s linear; renders the list twice + translateX(-50%) for a seamless loop; animates on ALL viewports; reduced-motion degrades to a static row.

sections.purpose: The one memorable thing: Amrock modernises older homes (sockets, lighting, heating), fits storage heaters and electric fires, and explains the work as it goes, to regulations. The copy boldness moment.
evidence_anchors: reviews[0] Molly (older property, additional sockets, light fittings, car charger; Naz + Henry)
reviews[1] Faye (explained clearly, up to regulations)
sections.copy_intent: 3 short, concrete statements of the specialism, stated plainly and factually (no boast adjectives). Each tied to real evidenced work: older homes brought up to standard; storage heaters and electric fires fitted and connected; explained as we go, to regulations.
sections.visual_intent: --stone section; large Inter Tight statements separated by brick-course rules; terracotta used once on the key phrase ('explained as we go'). No icons. The boldest copy moment on the page. Stats band (5.0 / 27 / Naz & Henry two-man team) inline.

sections.purpose: Name the 4 real services from evidence with specific, grounded descriptions.
evidence_anchors: site-strategy.json real_services (additional sockets & lighting; electric fires & storage heaters; EV & car chargers; extra circuits & power to outbuildings)
sections.copy_intent: Each service: short label + one evidence-grounded line. NEVER the broad Google labels ('Repairs and maintenance', 'Installations', 'Emergency callouts'). NEVER 'Emergency callouts' / 24-hour emergency (not evidenced). NEVER 'X services explained plainly'.
sections.visual_intent: Labelled rows with small two-letter marks (SL / EF / EV / OB) in terracotta - NOT 01/02/03 (services are not a sequence; the numbered process below is) and NOT SVG/lucide icons (no_service_icons check). Brick-course rule between rows.

sections.purpose: Show the one usable finished install - the proof photo that matters for a trader with no third-party profiles.
evidence_anchors: brief.json photos[1] (02-places.webp - completed_project: a finished living-room media wall with an electric fire installed, tidy, matches the heritage angle and Simon's electric-fire review)
sections.copy_intent: Short section intro; one safe caption only ('Completed living-room install, Cardiff'). No invented customer/location. ONLY 02-places.webp is used: 01-places.webp is work-in-progress (banned) and 03-places.webp is the brand logo, not a project photo.
sections.visual_intent: Single featured image with a thin --line frame and caption beneath; the masonry grid CSS degrades cleanly to one column. Quiet.

sections.purpose: Exactly one review section. Lead with the older-home / electric-fire / storage-heater quotes that carry the heritage angle.
sections.copy_intent: 3-4 verbatim Google excerpts, attributed 'First name, Google review'. JT1988JT is attributed 'Google review' (a username, not a real first name). No first name as a headline (no 'Steve' anti-pattern). One review section only (no duplicate wall).
sections.visual_intent: Quiet cards on --stone; terracotta quote rule; the 5.0/27 restated once as a stat, label 'Google rating' (no banned sub-labels).

sections.purpose: Reduce risk: show the orderly, well-communicated way Amrock works.
evidence_anchors: reviews[1] Faye (explained everything clearly, safely, up to regulations)
sections.copy_intent: 4 ordered steps grounded in review behaviour: get in touch (WhatsApp/phone) -> look at the job and a clear price -> work done safely to regulations, kept informed -> tested, left tidy and explained.
sections.visual_intent: Numbered 01-04 IS appropriate here (a real sequence). Numerals in heavy Inter Tight, terracotta; brick-course dividers between steps. Dark --ink section for contrast.

sections.purpose: Establish locality and availability without exposing the home address.
sections.copy_intent: Based in Cardiff (CF23), covers Cardiff and the surrounding CF postcodes (8-14 districts). CF23/Cardiff evidenced; rest derived from Cardiff geography (plausible one/two-van radius, no over-claim, no national footprint). No 24-hour hours line (the 'Open 24 hours' is a Google default and emergency work is not evidenced) - frame availability simply ('Most days, including evenings, by arrangement') OR omit a specific hours claim. No street address.
sections.visual_intent: Keyless Google Maps iframe centred on 'Cardiff CF23' (town + outward only); area list (two-column) beside framing prose.

sections.purpose: Convert: a quote form plus phone, anchored at #quote.
sections.copy_intent: Two columns. Left: heading + framing prose + key details (phone click-to-call, Cardiff / CF23). Right: quote form (name, phone, email optional, postcode, job type, details). Job types per assignment: Older-home upgrade / Additional sockets & lighting / EV charger / Consumer unit / Other. Heading specific to Amrock. Form is presentational only (does not submit to the business; no 'preview form' disclaimer).
sections.visual_intent: Dark --ink surface, NOT the terracotta accent (contact section rule). Form fields visually distinct from the side details. Mobile: stack to single column, details first. Quote-only sticky CTA reveals after hero on scroll (fade+slide), hides near footer.

sections.purpose: Real local-business footer, not just the agency credit.
sections.copy_intent: Brand, phone, areas, quick links, link to read Google reviews. Small 'Website by WebForTrades' credit at the bottom.

omitted_defaults: fabricated stats band beyond 5.0/27 + the two-man team (only Google 5.0/27 sourced)
'Emergency callouts'/24-hour service (not evidenced; the 'Open 24 hours' is a Google default)
rewires / consumer-unit upgrades as advertised completed services (not named in any review; consumer unit appears only as a quote-form request option)
a specific hours line claiming 24/7 availability
omitted_reasons: Kept lean and evidence-only. No section exists without a specific anchor in the frozen brief. The 'Open 24 hours' Google default is deliberately NOT surfaced as an emergency/24-7 claim.
